ety: ćhān˚ = Prk. छन्न(इ), or छन्ने(इ), fr. S. स्यन्न (p.p.p. of rt. स्यन्द्)
def: v.t. To sift; to strain, to filter; — to search minutely, to investigate; to canvass; to explore
more: v. a. S. चालन making to go. 1. To sift; strain; filter. Sote haiṅ apne muṅh pe ḍupaṭṭe ko tān-kar, Dete haiṅ ham ko sharbat-i-didār chhān-kar . Whene'er she sleeps her mantle's close drawn o'er her face, Clear sharbat straining through I drink the while I gaze. 2. To search, investigate, or examine closely ( jāṅchnā ); dive into; analyze. Phr. To look into every hole and corner; to beat the bushes; to leave no stone unturned. 1. Sārā daftar chhān ḍālā, par woh kāgaz na milā . 2. Yā ho najūmī kāmil, tāroṅ ko chhān ḍālā, Sūraj gahan bichārā, chandar gahan nikālā . On Death. Nazīr. 3. Sau mulk chhān āte haiṅ kauṛī ke wāste, Masjid ko dam meṅ ḍhāte haiṅ kauṛī ke wāste . On Kauṛis . Nazīr. A hundred countries sifted are all for pelf's sake, And even sacred fanes destroyed their pence to rake. chhān bīn , n. f. Close search; scrutiny; investigation; inquisition; exploration; analysis and synth…
v. a. 1. To strain, percolate, filter, sift. 2. To search, canvass, investigate. چهان لينا chhān lenā, To cull, to select. چهان مارنا chhān mārnā, To search, to rummage, explore, canvass.
subs: v.t. To sift, &c. ( = ćhānnā); — to cull, to select

To quarrel; fight. Āj hamārī unkī khūb chhanī .

def: s.f. Thatching, roofing, covering; the art of thatching; — huts or barracks for soldiers, a cantonment
more: n. f. H. chhānā to cover. 1. Thatching; the art of thatching. 2. A cantonment; barracks or huts for soldiers. chhāonī chhānā , v. n. lit . to thatch a roof. To live abroad, or away from home. Hai jinkī sej sūnī aur khālī chār-pāī Ro ro unhoṅ ne har dam yeh bāt hai sunāī; 'Par-desī ne hamārī abke bhī sudh bhulāī; Abke bhī chhāonī jā par-des hī meṅ chhāī . Nazīr. And those who on a lonely couch are doomed to lie, From hour to hour their sorrows tell and weep and cry, 'My husband still abroad, no word he sends to me, A home he's made himself in some far off countrie.' chhāonī ḍālnā , v. a. To make a cantonment. Angrezoṅ ne Qillāt meṅ chhāonī ḍāl lī .
(see چهانا chhānā ) s. f. 1. Cantonments, barracks or huts for soldiers. 2. Thatching, the act of thatching.

To be in hiding. chhup chhup ke , adv. Secretly. Ham se chhup chhup-ke jo kar lātī hai ek yār, nayā, Yeh chalan kab se nikālā, arī murdār! nayā? Wom. chhupne kī jagah, chhupāo , n. f. Hiding place; concealment.
v. n. 1. To be stamped. 2. To be printed; published. chhapvānā, chhapā'nā , Caus. of چهاپنا q. v. chhapvā'ī, chhapā'ī , n. f. Printing, or its cost.
v. n. To be concealed, to be hidden, to be absent, to disappear, to lurk. چهپ جانا chhip jānā, To retire, to withdraw.
v. n. To be printed or stamped.
wety: Inherited from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀙𑀼𑀧𑁆𑀧𑁆 (*chupp). See there for more.

def: v.t. To stamp, to seal; to print, bring out an impression, to publish
more: v. a. To print; bring out an impression; publish.
v. a. To print, to stamp.
wety: Urdu verb set چَھپْنا (chapnā) چھاپْنا (chāpnā) چَھپْوَانا (chapvānā) Inherited from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀙𑀧𑁆𑀧𑁆 (*chapp) + ـنا (-nā, verbal suffix).

def: s. m. ( lit. right and left) 1. Unsteadiness, want of principle. 2. Carelessness.
n. f. An old worn-out cap; a hat caved in. Pāoṅ meṅ jūti, na sir pe chapoṭī . Prov. Nor shoes for his feet nor cap for his head.
v. a. (dakh.) To strike the slipper (on); to reject or abandon.
adj. Fifty-six.
more: adj. S. षटपञ्चाशत् Fifty-six. Bhej, chhappan kaṛoṛ kī chauthāī, jo hāthī ke haude meṅ ghuṭe ṭhaṅḍāī . Bacchanalia. Bhaṅgaṛs . Send, send me just of millions fifty-six a quarter, An elephant I'll sit and swill bhaṅg ground in mortar.

def: s.m. incorr. for ćhappar, q.v.
s.m. A sloping thatch, a thatched roof; a shed, a hut; — tester of a bed); — (fig.) a heavy burden
more: n. m. S. छदि 1. A thatched roof; a shed. Chhappar par phūṅs nahīṅ, deoṛhī par nagāṛā! E. Prov. No straw on his thatch-roof and drums at his door! (Living for appearances). 2. A hut; a cottage. 3. A heavy load; calamity. Mere sir chhappar na rakkho. chhapar-baṅd , W.; gharāmī , E.; n. m. A thatcher. chhapar-bandī , n. f. 1. Thatching. 2. Cultivating, with permanent residence in a village. chhappar par phūṅs na honā , v. n. ( Met .) To be extremely poor or badly off. chhappar par rakhnā, yā dharnā, bālāë tāq rakhnā , v. a. To lay aside; resign; part with; fling aside as worthless; to disregard. Phr.—To lay on the shelf; lay up in a napkin; lay up in ordinary. 1. Ham aisī dostī ko chhappar par rakhte haiṅ . 2. Nahīṅ kuchh sūjhtā apne maze ke āge, aë bāṅdī, Mujhe chhappar pe rakkhā, ḍhūṅḍ tāī jā-ke yār apnā! Wom. Jān Sāhab. For your own pleasure only, slave girl, you have eyes to see, You've…
s. m. A thatched roof. چهپربند chhappar-band, s. m. A thatcher. چهپربندي chhappar-band-ī, s. f. Thatching.
subs: s.f. Thatching; price of thatching; cultivating, with permanent residence (in a village)
v.t. 'To place on the thatch,' to throw aside as worthless, to lay aside, to care nothing for
(-ko), 'To cleave the roof and give'; to surprise (one) with an unexpected gift; to give in a miraculous manner
'Roof to fall in'; a sudden calamity to befal (one)
ćhappar ḍālnā, To thatch; to make a roof or home (for oneself), to take up (one's) abode, to abide, to tarry
ćhappar-khaṭ, ćhapar-khaṭ, s.m. A bedstead with a tester and curtains.

def: s. m. A bedstead with curtains.
1. adj. Brazenfaced. 2. s. m. Slippers.
n. Cow-dung cakes. See uplā .

ety: ćhap, q.v.+rī, Prk. अडिआ = S. र+इका
def: s.f. A puddle, a pool.

adj. S. चिपिट Flat. Nīche se chapṭī, ūpar se gol; Chaḍh gaī naṭnī, baj gayā ḍhol . Riddle. Ans. A Huqqā . Quite flat below and round above, the acrobat Mounts to the top, and soon the drum goes rat-a-tat. chapṭā , n. m. Dim. chapaṭyā , n. f. 1. A large earthen vessel; a jar. 2. One having a flat nose. chapṭā golā , n. m. ( Math .) An oblate spheroid.
v. a. To flatten.

def: s.f. A seal or mark of cow-dung placed on a heap of grain on the threshing-floor to prevent its removal.

n. f. Congressus libidinosus duarum mulierum. chapaṭ-bāz, chapaṭhāī , n. f. Fœmina libidini sapphicœ indulgens. chapṭī laṛānā yā khel nā , v. n. Libidini sapphicœ indul gere. Shām guzar gaī, yār nā āyā, rāt bhī ādhī ān ḍhalī, 'Āo paṛausan, chapṭī kheleṅ, baiṭhe se begār bhalī! ' Nazīr.
s.f. Printing; cost of printing ( = ćhapāʼī).
v.t. To get or have printed, &c. ( = ćhapānā, q.v.). v.t. To cause to be stamped, marked, or printed, to get or have printed ( = ćhapwānā).
more: (caus. of چهاپنا) v. a. To cause to print.

ety: Prk. छारो and छारू; S. क्षारः and क्षारं
def: adj. & s.f. Caustic, corrosive; pungent; acid; saline; — alkali; ashes; dust, earth
more: n. f. S. क्षार, Pālī, chharikā . 1. Ashes ( rākh ); earth. Hīrā paṛā bajār meṅ, rahā chhār lipṭāe, Mūrakh mūrakh dekh rahe, chātur līyā uṭhāë . Kabīr. 2. A precipitous bank.
s. f. A large clod of earth.
subs: s.m. 'A pool of acid or saline mud,' name of a hell.

s.f. = ćhāṛ, q.v.

s.m. 'A pool of acid or saline mud,' name of a hell.

more: s. f. A bank of a river. چهاڙ چٿهي chhāṛ-chiṭṭhī, s. f. A permit, a pass.
subs: s.m. A medicinal plant, spikenard; a kind of fragrant moss, &c. (see ćhaṛelā).

def: s.f. Leaving, abandonment ( = tyāg); bank (of a river); land gained from a river, alluvium
subs: s.f. A permit, pass, passport ( = ćhoṛ-ćiṭṭhī).

def: s.f. = ćhaṛ, q.v. adj. & s.f. Caustic, corrosive; pungent; acid; saline; — alkali; ashes; dust, earth
more: n. f. 1. A long pole or bamboo. 2. A fishing rod. 3. Spikenard.
s. f. 1. Spikenard. 2. Shaft, pole, pike, staff, flagstaff, flag. See چهڙ.
subs: s.m. 'A pool of acid or saline mud,' name of a hell.

def: s.f. Spikenard.
more: s. f. 1. The pole of a spear. 2. Spikenard.

adv. Nolens volens ; perforce; inevitably.
more: adv. Certainly, inevitably, by force, right or wrong, nolens volens.

def: s.m. An ear-ornament made of pearls.
s.m. Ashes, &c.; — a lump; a large clod.
adj. (f. -ī), Separated, separate, apart; solitary, single, alone ( = ćhaṭwāṅ; ćhaṛīlā)
more: s. m. An ornament made of pearls worn in the ear.

s. f. 1. The price paid for pasturage. 2. Pasturage, grazing.
adj. = ćharahrā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Worn, emaciated, thin, lean, spare (man).
v.n. To be startled (an animal), be scared (syn. bhaṛaknā).
more: v. a. To sprinkle. چهڙک کربيچنا chhiṛak-kar bechnā, 1. To sprinkle water on stale or faded vegetables or fruits to make them appear fresh. 2. To puff off goods.

def: adj. Grazing (an animal). s. m. A beast, an animal that grazes. چرند و پرند charand o parand, s. m. Beasts and birds.
s. (dakh. from چرنا for چڙهنا) Mounting, ascending.
s. (dakh.) A small wound.
v. a. See چرانا charānā.
s. f. (dakh.) Ingenuity.
v. a. To chatter, to prattle.

n. m. 1. A squint-eyed man. 2. A low or mean fellow.
adj. Fourfold.
n. f. Alluvial land; alluvion.

v. n. 1. To advance; gain ground. Phr. To get the better of; get the upper hand; have the best of it; gain the ascendancy; obtain a victory; have it all one's own way; have a run of luck; run on all fours; sail before the wind; swim with the tide. 2. To score ( jītnā , 2). chaṛhtā bhāo , n. m. An advance or rise in price. chaṛhtā paṭṭā , n. A lease for a term of years at a progressively increasing rent. chaṛhtā din . Advancing day. chaṛhtā h. , v. n. To excel; rise; to be higher in quality or price. chaṛhtī kalā , n. Advancement; improvement.

n. f. 1. Ascent ( chaṛhāo , 1.). Abke Mansūrī par kam chaṛhāī huī . 2. Invasion; attack; assault. Gar dekhnī hai tujh ko kuchh aish kī chaṛhāī, Jo ek dam to terā ghar gūhme chhappar hāle. Uchhleṅ divāl pākhe, bhinnāve chārpāī, Pī āshaqoṅ meṅ ā-kar do bhaṅg ke piyāle! Nazīr. Would you enjoy high bounding ecstasy, Clear walls and see your bed spin where you lie? Two cups of bhaṅg with wassailers drink free, The house whirls round, the roof arocking see!
(from چڙهنا) s. f. 1. Ascent, acclivity, embarkation, attack, assault, irruption, rank, dignity. 2. Price paid for ascending, riding, embarking, &c.

v. a. Opp. of utārnā . S. उच्चर See چرَهنا 1. To lift; raise; take up. Asbāb chaṛhānā . 2. To drink; imbibe; quaff. Loṭe ke loṭe chaṛhā jātā hai . 3. To praise up to the skies; magnify; beslaver. 4. To dedicate; devote; sacrifice. fauj chaṛhānā , v. a. To lead an army. chaṛhāū , adv. Payment in advance.
s. m. A passenger, supercargo.

n. m. 1. Ascent; rise; elevation; acclivity; ascension. 2. Increase ( baṛhotrī , 1.). 3. Flood-tide; swell; inundation. 4. Intoxication; drunkenness. 5. A fit; paroxysm. 6. Passion; excitement; rage. 7. Loftiness; vain-glory; vanity; pride. 8. ( Math .) Involution; raising the power of a quantity (A. saūd ). cha hāo utār , n. m. 1. Ascent and descent; up hill and down dale; ups and downs. 2. Modulation of the voice; ( Mus .) rising and falling scale. 3. Involution and evolution (A. saūd o nuzūl ).
s. m. Ascension.
n. m. 1. An offering; libation; sacrifice. 2. The bridegroom's presents to the bride.
v. a. To find an opportunity.
adv. Avowedly, designedly, excellently.

v. n. Opp. of utar'nā . 1. To ascend; climb; mount; ride. 1. Hāthī par to chaṛhe khaṛe ho, ab kyā bāṅs par chaṛhoge? Āzād. Already on an elephant raised high, do you want a bamboo for a perch sky-high (like an acrobat)? (Reported to have been said by an Azād to Dyce Sombre who asked him for a blessing). 2. Chhātī par chaṛh-kar lahū pī jāūṅgī . Wom. I'll sit on your breast and suck all the blood out of you. (The angry mother to her child). 3. Mūā chaṛhā chalā ātā hai . Mah. Wom. Death take the wretch! he will come here. (He intrudes on her privacy). 4. Chaṛh mār, gūlar pakke . Prov. Get up, the fruit is ripe for you. (The ball is at your feet. Or, The game is in your hands). 2. To soar; fly; to be high up in the air. 3. To rise or be swollen (a river). 4. To rise (in price or value). Anāj bahut chaṛh gayā . 5. To be raised (the voice in singing). 6. To rise in the mind (idea or emotion). 1. Birhā jab se man chāḍho, jab se gayo jahān; Jaise jān nahīṅ loth meṅ jīvat hūṅ bin jān . Dohā. E'er since my love me left the world to me is naught, All sense of feeling lost as life in me were not. 2. Kabhūṅ man raṅg, taraṅg chaṛhe, Kabhūṅ man chāhat hai dhan ko . Kabit. At times the mind with transport glows, At times the greed of wealth foreshows. 7. To stand upright; stand up. 8. To be strung up, hanged, impaled. 9. To be raised, exalted, promoted. Chaṛhegā so giregā . Prov. Who climbs falls. 10. To be presented, offered, sacrificed. 11. To be strung (a bow), tightened, braced (the strings of a musical instrument). 12. To embark; go on board. 13. To top; to pair (as birds). Tante haiṅ agar aiṅṭh-ke chalte haiṅ ajab chāl, Jo pāoṅ kahīṅ, rāh kahīṅ, saif kahīṅ, ḍhāl, Kheṅcheṅ haiṅ kahīṅ bal, kahīṅ toṛ līyā gāl, Chaṛh baiṭhe kahīṅ, hāth kahīṅ, muṅh ko dīyā ḍāl, Is ḍhab ke maze rakhtī hai, aur raṅg javānī . Nazīr. With chest thrown out and side put on, youth moves a tip-top swell, His road is here, his feet are there, sword, shield awry as well, Now some one's hair is pulled, now bruised cheeks a tale might tell, Now this or that one tops, hands all about, lips lips compel, Such are the pleasures rich and rare which in young bosoms well. 14. To be put in the scale. 15. To be restored to its place, set (as a bone). Haḍḍī chaṛh gaī . 16. To march; go in a procession. Dhūm se barāt chaṛhī . 17. To grow; expand; to be enlarged. 18. To be superior; to exceed; excel. 19. To be practised in, dextrous, apt, accomplished. Hāth chaṛhā huā hai . 20. To get to the head; circulate; take effect (spirits, poison). Zardā chaṛh gayā . 21. To be spread over, put on, coated, enwrapped, encased. Raṅg chaṛhnā . 22. To be painted, gilded, varnished, plastered. 23. To pass; lapse; expire. Pahar bhar din chaṛhā . 24. To march against; assault; attack. 1. Ganīm chaṛh āyā . 2. Bukhār chaṛh ā . 25. To be exported or imported (as goods.). Nāj pūrab ko chaṛhtā hai . 26. To possess; influence; have power over. Iske sir jin chaṛhā huā hai . 27. To be put on the fire. Kāṭ kī haṅḍiyā bār bār nahīṅ chaṛhtī . Prov. You cant put a wooden vessel on the fire twice. (I am not to be sold twice). 28. To be put down, inserted, registered, booked. 29. To go or be dragged into court; to have recourse to law. Kachehrī chaṛhna . 30. To be in course of preparation; to be put in hand. Tumhārā hī kām chaṛhā huā hai . 31. To be contained in; to fit. 32. To fall into one's clutches. Ḍhab pe chaṛh gayā . 33. To move faster; to gain (a watch). 34. To be puckered, rumpled, wrinkled. Ham se garīb gurbā kīchaṛ meṅ gir paṛeṅ haiṅ, Hāthoṅ meṅ jūtiyāṅ haiṅ, aur pāëṅche chaṛhe haiṅ . Nazīr. And men of low degree like me all in the gutter fall, Their shoes are carried in their hands, tucked up their trousers all. 35. To be added on; to increase; fall in arrears. Qarz yā tankhuā chaṛhnā. chaṛhā-uprī , n. E. Bidding at an Auction. chaṛh nā utar'nā , v. n. 1. Ascending and descending. 2. Congressus libidinosus. chaṛhā baṛhā , n. m. A wealthy or great person.
v. n. To excel.

n. f. Increase; rise; advance; gain; premium. chaṛh'tī, chaṛhtā , adj. 1. Increasing; advancing; progressive. Chaṛhtī kalā, jāgtī jot . Prov. As waxing moon may you brighter shine! 2. Accelerated; increasing in speed; quick. 3. Rising; growing; flourishing; blooming. Chaṛhtī javānī . The ripening bloom of youth. 4. Better; superior. Is se bhī chaṛhtī .
n. m. 1. One who ascends or mounts; a rider ( chaṛhait ); climber. 2. One who places the grain, etc. in the scale for weighment. 3. ( Slang ) The rider; a lover ( chaṛhait ).
adj. A heeled shoe or slipper.
v. a. Caus. of chaṛhānā , q. v. chaṛh'vī , n. f. Raising rents. Carnegy .

ety: S. छटा; or dim. of ćhār
def: s.f. A lump (of unalloyed silver, or gold, &c.).
more: n. f. A lump of unalloyed silver or gold.!

def: s.f. = jhāṛī, q.v. s.f. A pitcher with a long neck and a spout to it, an ewer.

s.f. A switch, stick, wand, rod, cane, a walking-stick; — a procession of the followers of Shāh Madār; — ćhariyāṅ, pl. A kind of embroidery resembling a branch with twigs and shoots; — trimmings; stripes; — a Hindū marriage ceremony in which the bride and bridegroom playfully beat each other with sticks covered with flowers on the fourth day after marriage (see ćauthī)
more: s. f. A switch, wand, rod, cane. 2. A procession of the followers of شاه مدار shāh madār.
subs: s.m. The marshal who walks before people of consequence with the mace or wand of office, a mace-bearer ( = ćob-dār)
s.f. pl. Sticks covered with flowers.

def: s. f. (dakh.) Backbiting, talebearing: see چغلي chug̠ẖlī. چاڙي خور chāṛī-ḵẖor, Backbiter. چاڙي کهانا chāṛī-khānā, To backbite, &c.
adj. (f. -ī), Separate, &c. ( = ćhaṛā, q.v.). s.m. Ashes, &c.; — a lump; a large clod.
more: s. f. A kind of fragrant moss.
s. A kind of rock lichen, Lichen rotundatus, Rottl. See Qanoon-e-islam, glossary, p. ci.

def: s.m. Spikenard; — a kind of fragrant moss (cf. ćhāṛ-ćhaṛelā).

ety: ćhaṭ(nā)+indā = andā = Prk. अंतओ or अंदओ = S. अन्त+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Separate, alone, single, free, not burdened (a traveller), not loaded (cf. ćhaṭwāṅ; ćhaṛā; ćhaṛīlā).
s.f. pl. of ćhaṛī, q.v.
s. An article of dress so called, a vest without sleeves (?).
n. m. S. चर forage, कुट्ट cut. 1. The keeper's mate who cuts forage for the elephant. 2. A low fellow; a ragamuffin. Aise charkaṭe ham ne bahut se dekhe haiṅ .
s. (dakh.) An axle-tree.
adj. (dakh.) See فرتوت fartūt.
s. f. See خيال fānūs-i ḵẖiyāl under فانوس fānūs.

ety: ćhāṛ˚ = Prk. छड्ड(इ) = S. छर्द (ति), rt. छृद
def: v.t. To let go, release, &c. ( = ćhoṛnā, q.v.); — to vomit ( = ćhāṅṛnā, q.v.).
v.t. To separate (husk from rice, &c.), to beat or pound (rice), to husk; — to beat, chastise.

(intrans. of چهيڙنا chheṛnā, q. v.) v. n. To be touched, excited, played on.

def: n. m. Water in which an idol's, priest's, or husband's feet have been washed.
s. See چار چشم chār-chashm, under P. چار chār.
adv. Certainly, inevitably, by force, right or wrong, nolens volens.
s. f. 1. A bedstead, a bed. چارپاٴي پرپڙنا chārpāʼī par paṛnā, To get sick. 2. A litter for carrying the sick.
n. f. Pungency; flavour.
s. m. A quadruped. (Sans. चतुःपद ).
s.m. Small shot; a charge of shot, a shower of small pebbles, or balls, or nails, &c.
more: n. m. S. क्षुर, Pr. chhurang, chhuram . 1. A large knife; a dagger. 2. A razor ( ustarā ).

n. m. Small shot; shot. Musht-i-par ke vāste kyā chāhiye tīr o tufaṅg, Tujh ko kāfī hai faqat kaṅkar kā chharrā, fākhtah . Hasan. A handful of mere feathers needs no arrow sharp and keen, For shot mere pebbles just, O dove, would put your life out clean. chharrā uṛānā , v. a. To smoke madak , q. v. chharrā pilānā , v. a. 1. To load a gun with small shot. 2. To discharge small shot. chharrā chalnā , v. n. 1. To be thrown (pebbles, etc.) 2. To shoot. 3. To smoke madak q. v. 4. To shower jests.

adj. S. चट separate. Alone; solitary; unaccompanied. 1. Chhaṛī savārī kā kyā legā? 2. Chhaṛe ādmī kā kyā khānā? A single man wont want many dishes. chhaṛe chhaṭāṅk , adj. Singly; alone; by oneself. Aisī jagah par chhaṛe chhaṭāṅk jānā chāhiye .
s. m. 1. A large knife. 2. A razor. چهرا چهري chhurā-chhurī, s. f. Snick and snee, fighting with knives.
subs: v.n. To speak with animated and overpowering eloquence; — to smoke madak, q.v.
v.t. To load (a gun) with shot; to give (one) a charge of shot, or a shower of pebbles, &c.
v.n. Shot, or small pebbles, &c. to fly about, or be showered; jests to fly about; — to smoke madak.

def: n. f. Cont. of char/?/kh kī chūṅ . A creaking noise; creaking. Chal mere charkhe charra/?/kh-chūṅ, kahāṅ kī buṛhyā, kahāṅ kā tū? Prov. Move on my spinning-wheel—a humming all the way, What space divides you from the old crone who can say? ☞ The nursery tale relates how an old woman persuaded the tiger, and the leopard, and other beasts of the forest to defer eating her till her return from her daughter's house after she had grown plump and fat. On her way back she cheated the hungry expectants by concealing herself in a spinning wheel, repeating from within the above couplet in answer to the anxious enquiries of each beast of prey.
s.f. dim. of ćharrā, q.v.

more: n. f. chhat cover. 1. The breast (Sant. koṛam ); bosom ( sīnah ). 1. Chhātī par dhar-ke koī nahīṅ le-jāëgā . Prov. 2. Tū apnī chhātī par to hāth rakh! Wom. Just place your hand on your own breast! (Ask your own heart.) 3. Chhātī par chaṛh-kar khūn pī jāūṅgī . Wom. I'll sit on your breast and drink your blood. 4. Qaraz-dār chhātī par savār haiṅ . Prov. saying. The creditor sits upon the debtor's breast. 2. The breasts ( chhātiyāṅ , Sant. tovā ); bubbies ( chūchī ); dugs. 1. Koī kam-bakht milegā ā-kar, Kuchh phaṛaktī jo merī chhātī hai . Wom. Rangīn. Some wretch will surely come to meet me, I protest, For see my heart is fluttering so with felt unrest. 2. Bhādoṅ ghar jādoṅ nahīṅ, kahūṅ āj kyā batiyāṅ? Bin sajjan, sakhī, umag rahīṅ doū chhatiyāṅ . Birhā. It's August, and my spouse abroad, my woes how tell? Without my spouse, my dear, my two breasts bursting swell. 3. Spirit ( jurat ); enter…

To encourage. 2. To assure. چهاتي ٿهنڐي هونا chhātī ṭhanḍī honā, To be pleased or overjoyed. چهاتي جلنا chhātī jalnā, 1. To have the heart-burn. 2. To lament. چهاتي کا پتهر chhātī kā patthar, Nuisance, pest. چهاتي کا جم chhātī kā jam (lit. the destroyer of the heart) is applied to a person or thing the presence of which is unpleasant, a pest, a nuisance. چهاتي کهو…

adj. Courageous, spirited, brave, valiant; liberal, generous

To lick up with some honey (to make it go down). Āsāmī to mar gaī, tamassuk ko shahad lagā-ke chāṭā karo! The debtor is ruined, so you may spread honey on your bond and eat it.

To be cut, be clipped, be lopped, be pared, be pruned, be trimmed, &c.; to be cut taper, be nibbed (a pen); to be hewn down; to be hacked asunder; to be cut up (as an army, &c.); — to be picked out, be sorted; to be sifted; to be separated, be dispersed; to be cleaned; to separate; to be diminished, be reduced, be retrenched; to diminish; to waste away, become emaciated or thin, to decay (also written ćhaṅṭnā)
more: 1. s. m. A kind of sieve. 2. v. n. To decay, to separate, to be pruned, to be picked out.
v. n. To be adrift, to be discharged, to be got rid of, to be let go or left off, to be left or abandoned, to slip (from), to escape, to be liberated, to be loose, to be dishevelled (the hair).

def: s.m. An instrument for separating or sifting, &c., a kind of sieve.

ety: S. छत्त्त्रक+ār = Prk. आलो = S. आलः
def: adj. 'Umbrella-shaped' or 'mushroom-shaped'; bunchy, flat.

n. f. A kind of pickle of which the solid ingredients are bruised and retained with the vinegar; a mixture of salt and pepper with some acid for a relish; a sauce. 1. Harā dhanyā hai chaṭnī ko! Delhi street cry. Fresh coriander leaves for chaṭnī , ho! 2. Khāne meṅ chaṭnī, palaṅg par naṭnī . Prov. Sauce for meat, an acrobat in bed. ('Old fish at table but young flesh in bed.' Pope ). chaṭnī k. , v. a. 1. To bruise to chaṭnī , q. v. 2. To swallow as easily as sauce. Bheṛyā bakrī ko chaṭnī kar gayā . The wolf swallowed the goat as sauce is taken with meat. chaṭnī h. , v. n. 1. To be reduced to paste. 2. To be finished quickly. Tarkārī to chaṭnī ho gaī . The vegetables were licked up as so much sauce.
oblique plural of چَھت (chat)
n. m. A kind of dove.

adj. H. chāṭnā to lick. Toothsome; greedy. 1. Dekh jagat kī nār, kāmnī, tū to huī chaṭorī, Tū tiryā kam-jāt chhinalyā, khāve pān gilauri . Play. O beauty slim, sport for the world, that smackest night and day, Adultress base! thou chewest reddening pān to lure men gay. 2. Chaṭorī zabān, daulat kā ziyān . Prov. Toothsome taste, wealth waste. chaṭo'rā , n. m. A toothsome liver; one who feeds on dainties. chaṭoṛā-pan, chaṭor-pan , n. m. Toothsomeness; epicureanism. Chaṭorā khoë apnā ghar, batoṛā khoë dou ghar . Prov. Who smacks his chops all day his house in ruin lays, who tales invents doth others' houses raze.
s.f. Tossing about, fluttering; floundering; agitation, &c. ( = ćhaṭpaṭī).
(see چٿ پٿ) adj. 1. Active, hasty. 2. Stout. 3. Meat dressed with little sauce or gravy.
adj. 1. Tasty; savoury; hot; pungent. 2. Active; lively; smart; clever.
s. f. Agitation, flurry, flutter, palpitation.
s.f. Tossing about, floundering, fluttering; flutter, agitation, restlessness; briskness, activity; haste, hurry.
v.n. To toss or tumble about, to flounder, to flutter; to be agitated.
more: v. n. To toss, to tumble about, to flounder.
wety: From چھٹپٹ (chaṭpaṭ) + آ (ā, causative augment) + ـنا (-nā, verbal suffix).

def: (see چٿ پٿ) v. n. To be agitated, to flutter, to palpitate, to wince.
s. f. Haste, hurry, quickness, agility.
s.f. Tossing about, floundering, fluttering; flutter, agitation, restlessness; briskness, activity; haste, hurry.
adj. 1. Active, quick, alert, adroit. 2. Hot with spices.
s.m. A man of the second or Kshatrī caste ( = ćhatrī)
subs: s.m. A mere Kshatrī, a Kshatrī in name only, a base Kshatrī (a term of abuse).

def: s.m. A scholar, disciple, pupil, student; a tyro, novice.
more: s. m. Scholar, disciple, pupil, tyro.

def: s.f. A kind of fennel, Anethum sowā; anise; coriander; — a mushroom.
s.m. An umbrella (particularly a large umbrella held over idols and kings); — a cake of cowdung, or a stick, &c. placed on a heap of winnowed corn (in order to avert an evil eye)
more: n. m. 1. A large umbrella held over gods and kings. 2. A cow-pat; a stick, etc. placed on a heap of winnowed corn in order to ward off the evil eye. chhatr-patī, chhatr-dhārī , n. m. 1. One entitled to carry an umbrella. 2. A rājā ; king; prince. 3. God. Chhatr-patī, ghaṭe pāp, aur baṛhe ratī . God grant your sins be less, your fortune grow. (A blessing pronounced on one when he sneezes).

s. m. 1. ( छत्र ) An umbrella. چهترپت يا دهاري छत्रपति or छत्रधारी chhatr-pati or -dhārī, s. m. One entitled to carry an umbrella, i. e. a Rājā, prince, &c. 2. (corrupt. of چهيتر) A house set apart for the charitable entertainment of strangers, guests, &c.
s. m. (dakh.) A canopy, an hospital for the poor ( Harris ).
subs: s.m. 'Breaking or destruction of the royal parasol,' loss of dominion or empire, deposition; a forlorn condition; widowhood
s.m. 'Lord of the umbrella or parasol,' any king over whom a parasol is carried as a mark of dignity, a Rājā, king, prince
s.m. 'Bearing a parasol or umbrella,' a king, &c. ( = ćhatr-pati)
adj. & s.m. Possessing an umbrella; — one who has an umbrella ( = ćhattrī).

def: s.m. A mushroom.
s.m. The second or military caste of Hindūs; a member of the military or reigning class, a warrior, a soldier.
more: n. m. S. क्षत्रिय The second or military caste of the Hindus. Birāhman ho chorī kare, aur bidhvā pān chabāë, Chhatrī ho ran se phire to janam akārat jāë! Doha. A Brahman born and steal! a widow chewing betel leaf! A soldier born run from the field! all wasted life too brief!

n. f. छत्र 1. The hood or covering of a carriage, etc. 2. A tester or covering of a bed, etc. 3. An ornamental pavillion generally built over a place of interment, or a cenotaph in honor of a Rājā or Faqīr. 4. An umbrella ( chhātā ). Ghūm ghumerā laiṅhgā pahne, ek pāṅo se rahe khaṛī, Aṭh hāth haiṅ us nārī ke, sūrat uskī lage parī. Vāhid ne yeh kahī pahelī, dil meṅ apne soch zarī, Sab us se haiṅ ulfat rakhte, Banye, Bāman, aur Chhatrī . Riddle. Ans. An umbrella. A full wide skirt she wears, and on one leg she stands, Bright as a fairy she, behold outstretched eight hands, Vāhid this riddle asks, for answer think a wee, All do the maid affect, Vaish, Brahman and Chhatrī. 5. A bamboo frame for pigeons to settle upon. 6. Long hairs on the crown of the head.

1. s. f. ( छत्र ) A tester or covering of a bed, &c., a small umbrella. 2. A frame made of bamboos for pigeons to settle upon. 3. s. m. ( क्षत्रिय ) A man of the second or military tribe of Hindūs. See under برن baran ( वर्ण ). چهتري دار chhatrī-dār, Having a hood or covering, covered, hooded (a carriage, &c.).
s. f. A small basket without lid or handle.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit [Term?], from Sanskrit छत्र (chatra) / छत्त्र (chattra). Compare Hindi छाता (chātā), Nepali छातो (chāto), Punjabi چَھتْری (chatrī) / ਛਤਰੀ (chatrī).

def: s.f. A small umbrella, an umbrella ( = ćhātā); covering, canopy, or tester (of a bed); covering or hood (of a carriage, &c.); a small ornamental pavilion generally built over a place of interment, or a cenotaph in honour of a Hindū chief, or a faqīr; the poop of a ship; a bamboo frame (attached to the upper extremity of a long pole, for pigeons to settle upon); — long hairs on the crown of the head
subs: adj. Having a covering, or tester, or hood; covered, canopied, hooded (as a carriage, &c.).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit [Term?], from Sanskrit छत्र (chatra) / छत्त्र (chattra). Compare Hindi छाता (chātā), Nepali छातो (chāto), Punjabi چَھتْری (chatrī) / ਛਤਰੀ (chatrī).

def: adj. & s.m. Bearing or having an umbrella; — one who has an umbrella.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit [Term?], from Sanskrit छत्र (chatra) / छत्त्र (chattra). Compare Hindi छाता (chātā), Nepali छातो (chāto), Punjabi چَھتْری (chatrī) / ਛਤਰੀ (chatrī).

def: s.f. A woman belonging to the Kshatrī caste; the wife of a Kshatrī.
more: s. f. A female of the (چهتري) chhatrī cast.

def: s.m. A bee-hive, a honey-comb; a group, cluster; — a roofed lane, a covered way or passage.

s.m. A house set apart for the charitable entertainment of strangers, a choltry or resting-place; a shed, an open building.

n. m. A wooden toy for babies, consisting of a set of painted balls which are made to rattle together. chaṭṭe baṭṭe laṛānā , v. a. 1. To juggle with cups and balls. 2. To carry tales which set people by the ears. Tum ko khūb chaṭṭe baṭṭe laṛāne āte haiṅ .
adj. Thirty-six
more: = 1 × 20 + 10 + 6, adj. S. षटत्रिंशत, , Pālī. chhatimsā . Thirty-six. Hīnī puṛyā, chhattīs rog . Prov. Cheap drugs, diseases thirty-six. chhatīs bhojan , n. m. Thirty-six (a variety of) dishes.
subs: s.m. Thirty-six dishes of food ( = ćhattīs vyanjan); a variety of dishes

s.m. The thirty-six different kinds of food that are lawful to Brāhmaṇs.

def: Chhattisgarh (a state of India)

adj. (f. -ī), Cunning, artful, crafty; — ćhattīsī, adj. & s.f. Prudish, wanton, lewd; — s.f. A prude, an artful wanton woman, a strumpet (one who knows thirty-six postures in copulation and yet affects modesty and simplicity).
s.m. Artfulness, cunning, craftiness, wiliness.

n. f. S. चातुर्य dexterity. Cleverness; expertness; skill; accomplishments. 1. Dekho, dekho sajan, chatraiyāṅ hamār! Damṛī meṅ tīn kauṛī pher lāīṅ re . E. Wom. song. See, husband, aint I clever? only see, From farthing half I've brought back kauris three. 2. Torī Kaṅvar Kanhūī, niṭhurāī, chaturāī, mohe jān paṛī ab tanak tanak . Song. Thumrī . Your faithlessness and artful tricks, Kanhaiyā, Prince, I know now every bit. chatur'thī, sampradān , n. f. The fourth or dative case.

def: s.m. A house, a dwelling; an arbour, a bower.

more: s. m. Corrupt. of چـﮩبچه chah-bachcha, q. v.

def: n. m. Four taxes formerly levied under Native rule, viz. pāg, tāg, kūṛī , and puchchhī : i. e. pāg a turban (an adult), tāg a thread worn by a child round its waist, kūṛī a hearth, and puchchhī the tails of cattle. The first two correspond with the poll tax, the third with hearth-money (the fumage of Domesday Book). Carnegy .
n. f. Putting on new shoes (a horse). chau-bandī fasd , Bleeding in four places, i. e. the two arms and legs.
n. f. An obsequial rite performed on the fourth anniversary of a death.
adj. S. चतुर्विंशति Pālī, chatuvisatī . Twenty-four.
n. m. 1. A verse of four lines. 2. A play.

A kind of metre. It has in each line 30 instants with a pause at the 16th instant. Thus (4×4) + (4×3) + 2 = 30 instants. Kellogg. . chaupāl', chaupahal , adj. Four-sided (a brick). chau-pahrā . Four watches or twelve hours. chau-pher, chau-gird , adv. All round; on all sides. chau-pher nāp ( lit . four sides, conventionally, all the sides), A. masāhat-i-hudūd ; S. परिमिति Perimeter. chau-pahiyā , n. m. A four-wheeled cart. chautārā, chau-tār , n. m. A four-stringed musical instrument. chau-tāl, chautālā , n. m. A mode in music, the time of which is marked by four claps of the hands.
adj. Fourteen.
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi चौदह (caudaha), from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀘𑀉𑀤𑁆𑀤𑀲 (caüddasa), from Sanskrit चतुर्दश (cáturdaśa).

def: adj. Fourteenth. چودهوين رات chaudah-wīṅ rāt, Fourteenth night of the moon.

n. m. Crossing of the teeth in fight (elephants).
